required: Where exactly do you freeze on exams — at the first mechanism, or partway through?

effortModel.citation: Conditional: valid only with 2+ prior attempts AND user-confirmed concept understanding. Without these: use 4.0-4.5 min/page

expectedStrategy: Day 1-9: 55% skim+flag (known content fast), 45% timed practice problems (real exam conditions)
Day 10-12: full exam simulation — timer, book closed, one problem at a time
Day 13: debrief + relaxation ONLY — NO new content (anxiety increases with new material pre-exam)
Day 14: 30-min half-mock → rest, energy conservation for exam day
warnings: 2.5 min/page is CONDITIONAL: 2+ attempts + concept understanding confirmed — if 'don't understand this' signals appear, switch to 4.0 immediately
Day 13: absolutely no new content — this increases anxiety and disrupts retention
studying MORE does not fix performance anxiety — simulation practice does
alternatives: if concept gaps found: switch to TC-34 plan (4.5 min/page + mechanism drill)
performance anxiety track: breathing technique 2min before exam + 'I've done this before' anchor
hardFailChecks: must validate BOTH emotions separately: frustration (2× failed) AND panic (graduation risk)
must NOT prescribe 'study harder' for performance anxiety — knowledge is NOT the problem
Day 13 must explicitly be free of new content
2.5 min/page gating conditions must be verified before applying
must NOT give same plan as TC-33 or TC-34
toneExpectation: dual emotional validation first (frustration + panic separately); then pivot to knowledge vs performance anxiety distinction; calm strategic framing; GPT Pro rated this #1 hardest TC
